message:
feat(ast_matcher): expand Python destructive patterns with fixtures (git_safety_guard-beq)

Implements comprehensive Python pattern coverage per bead requirements:
- High-signal filesystem deletion patterns (Critical/High severity)
- Shell execution patterns with appropriate Medium severity
- 8 positive fixtures and 7 negative fixtures

Pattern additions:
- pathlib.Path().rmdir() - High severity
- Path().unlink() / Path().rmdir() - shorthand forms
- subprocess.call(), subprocess.Popen() - Medium severity
- os.popen() - Medium severity

Severity adjustments per bead guidance ("avoid code execution exists as default deny"):
- os.system() lowered from High to Medium (warns, doesn't block)
- All subprocess.* patterns are Medium (warns, doesn't block)

Test fixtures (git_safety_guard-beq acceptance criteria):

Positive fixtures (8 tests, must match):
1. shutil.rmtree - Critical, blocks
2. os.remove - High, blocks
3. os.rmdir - High, blocks
4. os.unlink - High, blocks
5. pathlib.Path().unlink() - High, blocks
6. pathlib.Path().rmdir() - High, blocks
7. subprocess.run - Medium, warns only
8. os.system - Medium, warns only

Negative fixtures (7 tests, must NOT match):
1. print('rm -rf /') - string only, not executed
2. import statements alone
3. Comments mentioning dangerous operations
4. Safe file operations (os.path.exists, os.listdir, open)
5. String variables containing dangerous-looking code
6. Docstrings mentioning dangerous operations
7. Note: shutil.rmtree DOES match structurally (path filtering is separate)

This enables accurate heredoc scanning for Python scripts while avoiding
false positives on common patterns like subprocess usage for safe commands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
